Bash(git -C /Users/djweiss/github/radarcheck add app.py forecast_table.py templates/table.html tests/test_forecast_table.py PLAN.md)

Bash(git -C /Users/djweiss/github/radarcheck commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Add forecast table view for simplified data inspection

Adds a table-based interface showing all forecast variables across all
time steps for a location. This provides a cleaner way to inspect raw
forecast values compared to the image-based views.

New files:
- forecast_table.py: Core table generator with CLI and library APIs
- templates/table.html: Responsive HTML table template
- tests/test_forecast_table.py: 34 integrity tests
- PLAN.md: Implementation plan with model comparison notes

New routes:
- /table/<location_id> - HTML table view
- /api/table/<location_id> - JSON API endpoint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Fix 2D coordinate handling for HRRR Lambert conformal projection

HRRR GRIB files use Lambert conformal projection with 2D lat/lon arrays.
The previous sel\(\) approach fails because latitude/longitude are not
indexed dimensions. This fix detects 2D coordinates and uses distance-based
nearest neighbor lookup instead.

Affected functions:
- cache_builder.py: extract_center_value\(\)
- app.py: extract_point_value\(\)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
Bash(# Check what data is available in cache ls cache/tiles/ne/*/)

Bash(# Get the last few entries of ASNOW for GFS curl -s ""http://localhost:5001/api/timeseries/multirun?lat=40.05&lon=-75.4&model=gfs&variable=asnow&days=7"" # Get the last few entries of PRATE for GFS curl -s ""http://localhost:5001/api/timeseries/multirun?lat=40.05&lon=-75.4&model=gfs&variable=prate&days=7"" # Compare echo ""=== GFS ASNOW ==="" cat /tmp/gfs_asnow.json)
Bash(# Check what tile files exist for latest GFS run ls -la cache/tiles/ne/0.1/gfs/)
